description_long: This house features a brick exterior with a prominent central glass bay that extends vertically, creating a greenhouse-like atrium at the front. The structure is two stories tall, with a steep gable roof and two tall chimneys. Large windows are symmetrically arranged on the sides and rear, while the front showcases a set of double glass doors flanked by tall windows. The house includes a covered carport or porch area on one side, and the rear facade is characterized by a simple, geometric arrangement of windows. The roof is primarily gabled, with glass elements incorporated into the central section, and the overall design blends traditional and modern architectural elements.
description: A two-story brick house with a central glass atrium, steep gable roof, and symmetrical window arrangement. Features include a covered porch, double glass entry doors, and a blend of traditional and modern design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central glass atrium, double glass doors, and symmetrical brick facade with a covered porch on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof, two chimneys, and the open carport or porch area beneath the house.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a simpler brick facade with large windows and the continuation of the glass atrium structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a geometric window arrangement and clear view of the gable roof and chimneys.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large central bay windows
attribute_entryway_design: Double glass doors set within a glass atrium, no steps, with a covered porch to the side and minimal decorative elements.
